root.name: Biological Disciplines
root.description: The life sciences organized by discipline — from molecular, cell and developmental biology through genetics, genomics, evolution and ecology to physiology, neuroscience, immunology, microbiology and the systems, structural and astrobiological frontiers. A deep authoritative map of biology's branches, their core theory, and their active research fronts.

pillars.name: Molecular Biology
pillars.scope: the molecular basis of biological activity; DNA replication, transcription, translation, gene regulation, RNA biology, molecular techniques

pillars.name: Cell Biology
pillars.scope: structure and function of cells; organelles, membranes and trafficking, cytoskeleton, cell cycle, signaling, cell death

pillars.name: Genetics
pillars.scope: heredity and variation; Mendelian and population genetics, linkage and mapping, mutation, epigenetics, quantitative genetics

pillars.name: Genomics & Bioinformatics
pillars.scope: genome-scale biology and computational analysis; sequencing, assembly and annotation, comparative and functional genomics, sequence algorithms, multi-omics integration

pillars.name: Developmental Biology
pillars.scope: how organisms build themselves; embryogenesis, morphogenesis, pattern formation, stem cells, regeneration, evo-devo

pillars.name: Evolutionary Biology
pillars.scope: the origin and change of biodiversity; natural selection, phylogenetics, speciation, molecular evolution, macroevolution, coevolution

pillars.name: Ecology
pillars.scope: interactions of organisms with each other and the environment; population, community and ecosystem ecology, biogeochemical cycles, conservation, global change

pillars.name: Physiology
pillars.scope: function of living systems; homeostasis, endocrine and nervous integration, cardiovascular, respiratory, renal and metabolic physiology across organisms

pillars.name: Neuroscience
pillars.scope: the nervous system and behavior; neurons and synapses, neural circuits, sensory and motor systems, plasticity, cognition, computational neuroscience

pillars.name: Immunology
pillars.scope: host defense and immune regulation; innate and adaptive immunity, antibodies and receptors, tolerance and autoimmunity, immunotherapy

pillars.name: Microbiology & Virology
pillars.scope: biology of microbes and viruses; bacterial physiology and genetics, microbial ecology and the microbiome, virology, host-pathogen interactions, antimicrobials

pillars.name: Structural Biology
pillars.scope: three-dimensional structure of biological macromolecules; protein folding, X-ray crystallography, cryo-EM, NMR, structure prediction, structure-function

pillars.name: Systems & Synthetic Biology
pillars.scope: quantitative and engineered biology; network and pathway modeling, gene circuits, metabolic engineering, genome editing, design-build-test

pillars.name: Biophysics
pillars.scope: physical principles of living matter; molecular motors, membrane and ion-channel biophysics, single-molecule methods, mechanobiology, statistical physics of biology

pillars.name: Botany (Plant Biology)
pillars.scope: biology of plants; photosynthesis, plant physiology and development, plant genetics, plant-environment interactions, crop and phytochemistry

pillars.name: Zoology (Animal Biology)
pillars.scope: biology of animals; comparative anatomy and physiology, behavior and ethology, animal diversity and systematics, physiological adaptation

pillars.name: Astrobiology & Origins of Life
pillars.scope: life beyond Earth and its beginnings; prebiotic chemistry, origin-of-life scenarios, extremophiles, biosignatures, habitability
